free_text: Resonant tunneling through the triple-humped fission
barrier of 236-U.
An enriched (99.89%), 88 mug/cm2 thick target of
235-U/2-O/3 on a 30 ug/cm2 thick carbon backing was
used.
The kinetic energy of the proton ejectiles was
analyzed with a Q3D magnetic spectrometer placed at 125
degrees (laboratory) relative to the incident beam,
covering a solid angle of 10 msr.
The position of the analyzed protons.
To detect fission fragments.
The experiment was carried out at the
Munich Accelerator Laboratory.
Proton-fission fragment coincidence.
An analysis is not given.
Hyperdeformed rotational bands. The excitation
energy of the ground state in the third minimum was
determined to be 2.7+/-0.4 MeV.  The height of the
inner fission barrier = 5.05 +/- 0.20 MeV and curvature
parameter = 1.2 MeV.
Table has been received from P.G. Thirolf.
Last checking has been done.
SD: Ref. AHP/N,18,323 added.
The excitation energy of the 236-U
compound nucleus can directly be deduced from the
kinetic energy of the outgoing protons.
-By author. DATA Contains the measured
high-resolution fission probability as a function of
the excitation energy of the compound nucleus 236U.  It
was  obtained  by  dividing  the proton energy spectrum
measured in coincidence with fission fragments by the
smoothly varying proton spectrum from the (d,P)
reaction.
-By compiler. Authors' data table contains zeros only
at excitation energy <3.04 MeV and >5.6567 MeV.
Data point uncertainty is shown as zero.
